headline: 7 cards in cards/ carry orphan archived_on: stamps from wrongful-archival restoration (6 known from token-collision incident 2026-05-26 + 1 earlier-collision-prior-art Ignite the Beacon WAR-18 from 2026-05-23)
summary: After the token-collision csv2mdbot bug (commit 4b570142) wrongly archived 6 enriched MTG cards on 2026-05-26 and they were restored from archive/ during the fix-and-recovery pass, the archived_on: 2026-05-26 frontmatter stamp survived on each card. Restoration moved the file back to cards/ but did not strip the cruft field. A seventh card — Ignite the Beacon WAR-18 — carries archived_on: 2026-05-23, evidence that the token-collision pathology fired in the 2026-05-23 ingest as well (which the run-history log corroborates: that run reported z=1 a=1, one card zeroed and archived; WAR-18 collides with WAR-18 Servo Token in the canonical printing). Archive/ currently holds only 2 unrelated files (MKM-249 Hustle&Bustle + MKM-250 Push&Pull); the 7 archived_on-stamped files in cards/ are all live, not archive shadows.

run_history_corroboration.2026-05-23 22:51:16: singles[c=157 u=2703 z=1 a=1 k=2860] — ONE card zeroed+archived (matches Ignite the Beacon archived_on: 2026-05-23)
run_history_corroboration.2026-05-26 23:17:11: singles[c=160 u=2854 z=6 a=6 k=3014] — SIX cards zeroed+archived (matches the 6 known restored cards' archived_on: 2026-05-26)
run_history_corroboration.2026-05-26 23:25:02: singles[c=41 u=3020 z=0 a=0 k=0] — zero zeroed (post-fix re-run; correct)

evidence.ignite_the_beacon_collision_pair: WAR-18 Ignite the Beacon (the parent card) collides with WAR-18 Servo Token (same set, same collector_number, different name) — classic token-collision shape per the commit-message bug description. Servo Token file exists at cards/magic-the-gathering/war-of-the-spark/18-servo-token.md (date_added 2026-05-27, landed in the patched re-run as one of the 41 net-new cards). Token-collision pre-dated the 5-26 incident by 3 days; the same bug fired silently.

fix.approach: Strip the archived_on: line from all 7 frontmatters. The field's semantic is 'this card has been archived'; for live cards in cards/, it is meaningless cruft. Preserve all other fields untouched.

source_side_prevention: csv2mdbot.py's restore-from-archive path (whether manual restoration or future automated recovery) should strip archived_on: as part of the move-back-to-cards/ operation. Alternative source patch: the post-aggregation sweep that flips needs_manual_review or zeros a card on archival should be the same step that, on un-archival, clears the archived_on field. Without source patch, future token-collision recovery (or any manual restoration) recreates the orphan.
neighbor_spotcheck: Confirmed archive/ holds only 2 unrelated files (MKM-249 Hustle&Bustle, MKM-250 Push&Pull) — no duplicates of the 7 cited files in archive/. Confirmed all 7 cited files are full-frontmatter live cards with current last_seen and market_price_as_of stamps. Cross-checked Scryfall canonical token-collision pairs from the commit message (CMR-8, M21-6, M21-12, M21-16, THB-2, ELD-3) — all 6 token-side files now exist as separate cards/ entries with date_added 2026-05-27 (the day-after-fix ingest), confirming the patched unique_key() now correctly splits parent-card and token-card files. The 41 net-new cards' silent-loss pattern: I spot-checked for an ELD-2 All-That-Glitters orphan (collides with ELD-2 Human Token) — neither in archive/ nor in CSV — so Alex doesn't own it, not a silent-loss casualty.
isolation_check: Pattern is isolated to the restoration aftermath; no other cards corpus-wide carry archived_on: outside the 7 cited files. Future-watch: any subsequent token-collision recovery pass should sweep for archived_on: residue.
